message:
feat(snapshot_engine): Cx-first shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds SnapshotEngine::shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx behind the
asupersync-runtime feature, extending the Cx-first migration pattern
already landed for EventWaiter::wait_with_cx (event_stream.rs),
NativeEventListener::run_with_cx (native_events.rs), WorkflowRunner,
SearchBridge, CautClient, SurvivalModel, MuxWatchdog, RestoreProcess,
TelemetryCollector, Tailer, IPC primitives, and the wait_for predicate
loop.

Semantics mirror shutdown_checkpoint with the inner timeout rebound via
runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx so outer-scope cancellation (operator
abort, deadline collapse) cuts the capture race deterministically under
LabRuntime virtual time rather than only responding to the explicit
timeout argument.

Pre-flight: if the caller's Cx is already cancelled on entry, the full
capture is skipped, the session is best-effort marked as cleanly shut
down (so restart does not offer an unclean-shutdown recovery prompt for
a session the operator intentionally ended), and the method returns
Ok(None). An operator who has abandoned the shutdown race should not
trigger a full capture just to immediately time out.

The legacy shutdown_checkpoint entry point is preserved for non-migrated
callers; this change is strictly additive.

Tests:

- shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx_pre_cancelled_marks_shutdown_without_capture:
  pins the pre-cancel contract — no capture attempt recorded, Ok(None)
  returned well under the timeout budget, mux_sessions.shutdown_clean
  still set to 1.

- shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx_happy_path_captures_and_marks: pins that
  a fresh, non-cancelled Cx behaves identically to the legacy path —
  captures a Shutdown-triggered snapshot and marks the session clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
